question: Regarding alert symptoms and causes, which of the following is true?
incorrect_answers: Causes are a better way to capture more problems more comprehensively and robustly with less effort
Causes are unimportant and can be ignored

question: What are the core components of Prometheus?
correct_answer: Prometheus, Alertmanager, Pushgateway, Client Libraries, Exporters

question: What does the Prometheus scrape interval represent?
correct_answer: How often to collect metrics for a scrape
incorrect_answers: Which targets to collect from for a scrape
How often to apply scrape metadata as labels
How long to wait before giving up on a scrape

question: What is a Prometheus metrics registry?
correct_answer: A set of metrics in an instrumented application that will be returned when scraped
incorrect_answers: A documentation site detailing all of the public exporters and instrumentations
A documentation site detailing the metrics provided by a specific exporter or instrumentation
A code library used for exposing Prometheus metrics

question: Which of the following functions is NOT appropriate for use with gauge metrics?

incorrect_answers: Temperature of a CPU in degrees Fahrenheit

incorrect_answers: Which of the following metric names and types is the MOST appropriate for this scenario?

question: In which of the following scenarios is distributed tracing NOT particularly useful?
incorrect_answers: A customer interaction with calls that span an internal data center and a public cloud such as AWS
A batch job that uses an API to make calculations and store the results in a database
A 3-layer web architecture with a frontend, backend and database

question: What is the purpose of Prometheus recording rules?
correct_answer: Evaluate a PromQL expression and save the result back to the TSDB as a new metric
incorrect_answers: Define criteria for recording details of metrics for later retroactive evaluation of alerting rules
Record the screen of users of an application and generate metrics from their session
Record the current state of all firing alerts for later analysis

question: What is the difference between the rate and irate query functions?
correct_answer: The rate function considers the entire range selector interval, irate only considers the last two samples in the range selector interval
incorrect_answers: There is no difference between the two functions
The rate function calculates an instantaneous rate, the irate function calculates an average rate
There is no such function called irate

question: Which of the following is the definition of a Service Level Objective?
correct_answer: A goal regarding an aspect of a service such as its availability
incorrect_answers: An agreement made between two or more parties about aspects of a service such as its availability

question: Which of the following is a valid way to trigger a Prometheus configuration reload?
correct_answer: Sending a SIGHUP to the Prometheus process
incorrect_answers: Sending an HTTP POST request to the /-/reload endpoint with the --web.enable-admin-api command line flag set
Sending an HTTP GET request to the /-/reload endpoint with the --web.enable-enable-lifecycle command line flag set
Sending an HTTP GET request to the /-/reload endpoint with the --web.enable-admin-api command line flag set

question: As a best practice, which of the following is a poor use of Alertmanager silencing?
correct_answer: Silence a specific alert for which notifications are no longer desired
incorrect_answers: Silence alerts from a server for brief period while it is being initialized
Silence alerts from a server that is being deprovisioned
Silence alerts from a set of servers which are undergoing routine maintenance

correct_answer: Alert labels should be used for metadata that uniquely identifies an alert while annotations should be used for longer-form descriptive content about an alert
incorrect_answers: Alert annotations should be used for metadata that uniquely identifies an alert while labels should be used for longer-form descriptive content about an alert

question: What does a span represent in regards to tracing?
correct_answer: A call or logical section within a larger transaction detailing response time, status code and other metadata about the call
incorrect_answers: Complete tracing coverage of all applications and services in an organization
An end-to-end flow of a transaction, including response times, status codes and metadata
Metadata about a call, including the source and destination IP and DNS names
